perceptual_narrative_logs.visual_narrative_log: (1) A young woman in white pants and a pink top walks toward the camera and then jumps onto a large round sofa in a modern living room. (2) She lies on the sofa, resting her head on her hand, with a box of fried chicken on the coffee table nearby. (3) A TV screen shows a parody of the 'Titanic' ship scene with the woman's face superimposed on the characters. (4) The woman eats a piece of fried chicken while crying dramatically; she wipes her face with a standard tissue, which leaves small white scraps all over her face. (5) She looks frustrated at the scraps and the grease on the table. (6) She then holds up large blue packs of Su-Jie brand tissue. (7) Close-up shots demonstrate the three-layer thickness of the tissue, her pulling it forcefully to show strength, and her using it to wipe the table and her face cleanly. (8) She drops a tissue into a toilet and it flushes away smoothly. (9) She introduces wet wipes of the same brand, comparing them to the dry tissues. (10) The video ends with her posing with multiple packs of the product and giving a thumbs-up.
perceptual_narrative_logs.audio_narrative_log: (1) Upbeat and energetic pop music begins with a loud 'thud' sound as the woman lands on the sofa. (2) The music shifts to a dramatic, orchestral style reminiscent of a movie soundtrack during the 'Titanic' parody scene. (3) Sounds of exaggerated sobbing and loud chewing noises are heard. (4) An energetic male voiceover enters, speaking in a professional and persuasive tone to describe the product features. (5) Specific sound effects include a sharp 'swish' or 'zip' sound when tissues are pulled or demonstrated. (6) The sound of a toilet flushing is clearly audible. (7) The upbeat pop music returns to a higher volume as the narrator concludes with a call to action.
perceptual_narrative_logs.text_narrative_log: (1) (完美降落) - Humorous caption describing her landing on the sofa. (2) (哭的咪咪茂茂) - Slang caption describing her messy crying. (3) (擦一下眼淚) - Functional text noting she is wiping tears. (4) (滿臉衛生紙葵渣?) - Questioning caption about the tissue scraps on her face. (5) 舒潔厚韌三層衛生紙 - Functional text identifying the product name. (6) 扎扎實實的三層 - Decorative text emphasizing the three-layer thickness. (7) 獨家交織技術 - Functional text about the manufacturing technology. (8) 強韌不容易破 - Decorative text emphasizing strength. (9) 可以安心丟馬桶 - Functional text stating it's flushable. (10) 衛生紙94要乾加濕 - Hook text promoting the use of both dry and wet tissues. (11) 全通路都買得到喔 - Functional text for availability.

temporal_pacing_summary.mainActionFlow: Problem Hook -> Dramatic Parody -> Product Introduction -> Technical Demonstrations -> Usage Scenarios -> Conclusion.

visual_human_presence.countingEvidence: 1 female actor appears as the main protagonist throughout the video.
visual_human_presence.mainCharacterDescription: An Asian woman with long hair tied in two braids, wearing a light pink tank top over a white dress or white pants.

visual_scene_and_style.backgroundDescribe: A modern, minimalist indoor space featuring a light-colored wooden floor, a grey circular sofa, a dark coffee table, and a clean white-tiled bathroom for the flush test.
visual_scene_and_style.lightingStyle: Bright, high-key studio lighting with a natural feel.

vocal_qualities.tone: Energetic and professional narrator; dramatic and emotional female character.

narrative_and_purpose.narrativeStructure: Montage: Hook -> Problem -> Solution -> Demonstration -> Outro
narrative_and_purpose.mainPurpose: This is a commercial designed to promote Su-Jie (Kleenex) 3-layer toilet paper and wet wipes. It uses a humorous 'problem' (tissue scraps on the face after crying) to highlight the product's superior strength and lack of residue compared to ordinary tissues, followed by technical demonstrations of absorbency and flushability.

endorsement_analysis.endorsementCues: The entire video is centered around showcasing the brand's packaging, logo, and product performance.

social_sociology_tags.uniqueSignatures: Fast-paced editing with vibrant text overlays and movie parody elements typical of modern social media advertising in Asia.
social_sociology_tags.freeDescribe: The video effectively utilizes a popular 'problem/solution' format common in influencer-led marketing to build trust and interest in a household staple.
